instincts.title: Add CLI UX regression tests with output changes
instincts.trigger: When changing any CLI command behavior (stdout/stderr text, exit codes, help output, error messages)
instincts.action: Add or update a focused CLI UX test that asserts exit code and a small set of stable substrings/lines (not full output), covering both success and failure cases; avoid brittle assertions on whitespace, ordering, or environment-specific paths unless required.

instincts.title: Treat loom-agile invariants tests as the contract
instincts.trigger: When changing loom-agile pack content (skills/agents) or any pack loading/validation logic that touches loom-agile
instincts.action: Read/align against tests/test_pack_loom_agile_core_invariants.py first; ensure skills and agent definitions satisfy the invariants instead of weakening the tests unless the contract is intentionally changing.

instincts.title: Prefer basedpyright over LSP diagnostics for Python gates
instincts.trigger: When you need to assess/resolve Python type issues in a repo that treats basedpyright as a required gate (or explicitly instructs to use it).
instincts.action: Run `uv run basedpyright` and fix reported issues; do not rely on editor/LSP diagnostics or the `lsp_diagnostics` tool as a substitute for the gate.

instincts.title: Avoid shipping skills that only describe proposing skills
instincts.trigger: When considering adding a skill whose primary purpose is to generate/propose other skills/instincts (meta-process rather than an engineering workflow)
instincts.action: Do not add it unless it encodes a repeatable, durable workflow that cannot live as instincts; otherwise keep the process in instincts and episodes, not as a user-invokable skill.

instincts.title: Do not ship editor/agent tool artifacts in templates
instincts.trigger: When adding or reviewing files under template trees like src/agent_loom/compound/opencode/ (or other scaffolded outputs).
instincts.action: Exclude tool-specific project files (e.g., `.serena/*`, local IDE metadata, assistant-runner configs) from templates and committed scaffolds unless they are a deliberate product feature; prefer removing them rather than ignoring via template-local `.gitignore`.

instincts.title: Add CLI UX tests alongside CLI behavior changes
instincts.trigger: When changing CLI output, exit codes, error handling, or flags for any loom subcommand (especially user-facing UX paths).
instincts.action: Add or update a focused pytest UX regression test that asserts exit code + the most stable stdout/stderr fragments (avoid brittle full-output snapshots), and include at least one negative case (bad args, missing resource, etc.).

instincts.title: New CLI nouns must ship with docs + help polish
instincts.trigger: When adding or expanding a `loom <noun>` CLI surface (new module, new commands, new flags)
instincts.action: Update the noun-level README (or equivalent) with 2-5 concrete usage examples; sanity-check `loom <noun> --help` for clarity and flag naming; add or update CLI UX regression tests when behavior is user-visible.

instincts.title: Use unified diffs for human-readable CLI change output
instincts.trigger: When adding a CLI command that shows changes between two text versions (config, generated output, previews, etc.)
instincts.action: Generate output via `difflib.unified_diff` with explicit `fromfile`/`tofile`, fixed context lines, and `lineterm=""`; print nothing (or a single friendly line) when no changes; keep diff generation pure so it is easy to test.

instincts.title: CLI UX Changes Need Targeted UX Tests
instincts.trigger: When modifying CLI output, help text, defaults, or command structure
instincts.action: Update/extend UX-focused tests to lock in the intended ergonomics and prevent regressions; avoid untested copy changes that silently degrade UX.

instincts.title: CLI UX Changes Need UX Tests
instincts.trigger: When you change CLI text output, flags, or exit-code behavior
instincts.action: Add/adjust a dedicated UX test that asserts exit code + key stdout/stderr lines for the affected command(s); keep assertions minimal but intentional (user-visible contracts only) so refactors don't cause noise.

instincts.title: Add CLI UX regression tests when output changes
instincts.trigger: When changing any CLI command output, flags, exit codes, or user-facing error messages (especially in ticket commands).
instincts.action: Add/extend a pytest UX test that exercises the command and asserts: exit code, stdout/stderr content (including key lines and spacing), and stable formatting. Prefer small, readable golden strings (dedent) over broad regexes; avoid asserting volatile data (timestamps, temp paths) unless you control/freeze it.

instincts.title: Add CLI UX tests for new commands
instincts.trigger: When adding or changing a CLI command/subcommand (new noun/verb, flags, output text, exit behavior)
instincts.action: Add/adjust a UX regression test that asserts (1) exit code, (2) key stdout/stderr lines, and (3) a stable error message for failure paths. Prefer partial/regex assertions and avoid brittle full-output snapshots unless output is explicitly stable.

instincts.title: Compound autolearn responses are JSON-only and tool-free
instincts.trigger: When acting as the background autolearn/compound learning agent producing memory-only updates.
instincts.action: Do not run any tools; output exactly one valid JSON object (no markdown). If there is no durable learning worth persisting, output `{}`. Keep within budgets and avoid proposing product code changes.

instincts.title: Use vendor-neutral wording in compound guidance
instincts.trigger: When writing skills/prompts/docs that will be reused across environments or toolchains
instincts.action: Avoid vendor/product-specific language and paths (e.g. naming a specific assistant or referencing `.claude/...`); write instructions in tool-agnostic terms that map to Loom/OpenCode primitives (`.opencode/skills`, `.loom/compound/...`, instincts/episodes) so guidance survives migrations.

instincts.title: Keep instincts docs and compiled JSON in sync
instincts.trigger: When adding or editing any Compound instinct.
instincts.action: Update `.loom/compound/INSTINCTS.md` and regenerate/adjust `.loom/compound/instincts.json` in the same change. Verify IDs are unique, kebab-case, and identical across doc + JSON; ensure any new instinct has a clear trigger/action and minimal tags.

instincts.title: Prefer consolidating skills over creating near-duplicates
instincts.trigger: When adding or revising skills under .opencode/skills/ and you notice overlapping scope or repeated procedure
instincts.action: Update the most canonical existing skill (better name, better adoption surface) and remove the redundant one; keep a single durable procedure per workflow.

instincts.title: Treat .opencode/skills as the canonical skill location
instincts.trigger: When reading, updating, or proposing skills for Loom / OpenCode
instincts.action: Prefer `.opencode/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` as the source-of-truth; if a parallel legacy path exists (e.g. `.claude/skills/...`), do not create/maintain duplicates—converge on the `.opencode/skills` copy and scrub references to legacy locations in guidance.

instincts.title: Keep compound instincts docs + JSON in lockstep
instincts.trigger: When adding/removing/editing a compound instinct (or changing its id/title/action)
instincts.action: Update both `.loom/compound/INSTINCTS.md` (human-readable catalog) and `.loom/compound/instincts.json` (compiled/consumed form) in the same change; ensure ids are kebab-case, stable, and referenced consistently across skills/episodes; avoid near-duplicates by merging semantics into one instinct when possible.

instincts.title: Keep compound install outputs deterministic
instincts.trigger: When changing compound scaffold/template contents or compound install logic (e.g., src/agent_loom/compound/scaffold.py, src/agent_loom/compound/paths.py, src/agent_loom/compound/docs.py, compound opencode template tree).
instincts.action: Treat the installed `.opencode/` (and any `.loom/compound/` docs) as a contract: preserve stable paths, stable file ordering, stable newline formatting, and stable rendered content; immediately update/extend `tests/test_compound_install.py` to lock the behavior.

instincts.title: New Subsystems Get A Scope README Immediately
instincts.trigger: Adding a new top-level package/subsystem (or formalizing an existing cluster of files into a new package).
instincts.action: Add a short README in the package directory that defines: what it is, what it is not, key entrypoints, and how to test it. Keep it intentionally small so it stays maintained.

instincts.title: Keep CLI output deterministic by default
instincts.trigger: When a CLI command prints lists, tables, multi-item sections, or derived metadata (IDs, paths, counts).
instincts.action: Ensure ordering is explicit and stable (sort inputs, stable iteration, deterministic grouping). Normalize or avoid non-deterministic content (timestamps, random IDs). Make newline behavior consistent (exactly one trailing newline; no extra blank lines unless intentional) and cover with a UX test.

instincts.title: Keep diff output stable and easy to snapshot-test
instincts.trigger: When writing tests for CLI diff output, or when diff output might be consumed in pipelines/logs
instincts.action: Avoid ANSI by default (or gate it behind TTY / `--color`); ensure deterministic file labels and context lines; normalize trailing newlines so diffs don’t flap; in tests, assert on a small number of stable sentinel lines (headers + one hunk) rather than the entire diff when possible.

instincts.title: Keep docs + core aligned with new subcommands
instincts.trigger: When introducing a new subcommand or expanding a subsystem CLI (e.g., `loom memory ...`)
instincts.action: Update subsystem README/help docs alongside the CLI wiring, and factor logic into a testable core module so UX tests can stay thin; ensure docs mention common workflows and error modes, and add at least one UX test covering the new path.

instincts.title: Document subsystem entrypoints and mental model
instincts.trigger: When adding a new subsystem or expanding an existing one with multiple new modules (e.g., `core.py`, `models.py`, `cli.py`, `recall.py`)
instincts.action: Add/update a subsystem README that explains: the user-facing commands, where data is stored, primary module responsibilities, and the quickest 'smoke path' to validate behavior.

instincts.title: Extract A New Package When A Module Collapses
instincts.trigger: A refactor produces a diffstat dominated by large deletions in a single file/module (hundreds of lines removed) and the remaining code is conceptually multiple responsibilities.
instincts.action: Extract a focused package with a small public surface (exports in __init__.py), split by role (models/types vs core logic vs registries), add a package-level README that states scope + non-goals, then update callers/tests to depend on the new package (not the old internal layout).

instincts.title: Git preflight: status + diff + log before commit/PR work
instincts.trigger: When asked to create a commit or a PR, or when preparing to stage changes
instincts.action: Run `git status`, `git diff` (staged+unstaged), and `git log -n ...` early to understand scope/style; avoid staging secrets and avoid destructive git operations unless explicitly requested

instincts.title: Avoid backcompat framing in greenfield code/docs
instincts.trigger: Writing docs, flags, deprecations, or migration behaviors in early-stage systems
instincts.action: Prefer clean breaks and simple interfaces; remove/avoid 'backcompat' language unless there is a real external user contract that requires it.

instincts.title: Hydrate wikilinks when writing Loom memory
instincts.trigger: When adding/updating Loom memory notes that contain `[[wikilinks]]` via CLI or core APIs
instincts.action: Resolve referenced wikilinks during the write path (not only at recall time): normalize link tokens, ensure referenced note ids exist (create placeholders if intended), and persist hydrated link metadata so recall/grep/backlinks are consistent; update golden tests to lock in the user-visible behavior.

instincts.title: Ignore new persistent artifacts in git
instincts.trigger: When introducing any persistent local artifact (sqlite db, index, cache dir, logs, tmp files) as part of a new subsystem
instincts.action: Update `.gitignore` to exclude the artifact(s) and add a short doc note describing where the artifact lives and how to reset it safely.

instincts.title: Align Core Errors With CLI UX
instincts.trigger: When updating core behavior that the CLI surfaces (validation, missing resources, ambiguous inputs)
instincts.action: Make core raise/return a single, well-typed/consistent error shape that CLI maps to stable user-facing messages + non-zero exit codes; add a UX test covering the error path.

instincts.title: Keep OpenAPI and Dashboard Consumers in Sync
instincts.trigger: When changing server routes, request/response shapes, or endpoint behavior
instincts.action: Update `docs/openapi.yaml` in the same change and verify any dashboard readers/clients that depend on those endpoints still work; treat schema drift as a bug.

instincts.title: Update CLI UX tests alongside CLI behavior changes
instincts.trigger: Any change to CLI output, flags, or subcommand routing
instincts.action: Locate and update the UX snapshot/expectation tests (e.g. `tests/test_*_cli_ux.py`) in the same change; ensure wording is intentional and stable.

instincts.title: Close Loom tickets, don't delete files
instincts.trigger: When cleaning up or finishing work tracked under `.loom/ticket/`
instincts.action: Preserve history by closing the ticket (or moving it into `.loom/ticket/closed/`) instead of deleting the markdown file directly; only remove a ticket file if it was created by mistake and contains no useful record.

instincts.title: Memory feature changes should co-evolve with README
instincts.trigger: When adding/changing Loom Memory behavior (notes merge, recall, indexing, CLI surface area).
instincts.action: Update `src/agent_loom/memory/README.md` to reflect the new behavior and examples, and ensure the docs match the CLI UX tests (examples should be testable expectations, not aspirational).

instincts.title: Use golden tests to lock memory CLI UX deltas
instincts.trigger: When changing memory note serialization, parsing, or CLI output formatting
instincts.action: Add/adjust a focused golden test that asserts the stable, user-visible output (stdout/stderr + ordering) for the exact scenario that changed; keep fixtures minimal and deterministic so future diffs are high-signal.

instincts.title: Keep OpenAPI spec deterministic and non-generated where possible
instincts.trigger: Editing `docs/openapi.yaml` during endpoint/schema updates
instincts.action: Avoid volatile fields like timestamps; keep component and path ordering stable; validate references and schema names stay consistent with the code.

instincts.title: Treat Pack UX + Core As One Contract
instincts.trigger: When making a pack behavior change that has both a core and CLI surface area
instincts.action: Update core logic and tests together: core/lifecycle invariants in `tests/test_pack_lifecycle.py`, user-facing contract in `tests/test_pack_cli_ux.py`; ensure failures are informative (actionable error messages) and consistent with expected exit codes.

instincts.title: Pack Changes Need Lifecycle Coverage
instincts.trigger: When you modify pack core behavior (selection, creation, install/activate, lifecycle/cleanup) or touch `src/agent_loom/pack/core.py`
instincts.action: Add/adjust lifecycle tests to lock in invariants (happy path + edge cases) in `tests/test_pack_lifecycle.py`; make tests assert durable behaviors (state transitions, idempotency, cleanup, and failure modes) rather than incidental implementation details; run `uv run ruff check .`, `uv run basedpyright`, then `uv run pytest`.

instincts.title: Sync pack sample + tests whenever pack code changes
instincts.trigger: When editing any pack implementation under src/agent_loom/pack/ (core/lock/packs/cli) or changing pack behaviors/validation
instincts.action: Update the reference pack at src/agent_loom/pack/packs/sample/pack.yaml and any sample command/docs under src/agent_loom/pack/packs/sample/files/ to match the new behavior, then adjust tests in tests/test_pack_lifecycle.py and tests/test_pack_loom_agile_core_invariants.py to reflect intended invariants.

instincts.title: Lock Pack CLI UX With Low-Noise Tests
instincts.trigger: When you change pack-related CLI commands (flags, help text, user-visible messaging, exit codes) or add new pack UX behavior
instincts.action: Add/update focused CLI UX tests in `tests/test_pack_cli_ux.py` that assert exit code and a few key stdout/stderr substrings; avoid brittle full-output snapshots; keep assertions resilient to formatting/ordering changes while still protecting the user contract.

instincts.title: Submit plan and wait for approval before implementation
instincts.trigger: When operating under Plan Mode / read-only constraints or when a plan is explicitly requested as a gate before coding
instincts.action: Do only inspection/analysis; produce a concrete, executable plan; call `submit_plan` with plan+summary; do not edit files or run write-capable tools until the plan is approved

instincts.title: Run Quality Gates Immediately After Refactors
instincts.trigger: After a broad refactor that touches many files and tests
instincts.action: Run `uv run ruff check .`, `uv run basedpyright`, then `uv run pytest`; treat failures as part of the refactor (not follow-up work).

instincts.title: Prefer basedpyright over LSP diagnostics
instincts.trigger: When you see/typecheck errors via editor/LSP or are about to declare a Python change 'done'
instincts.action: Run `uv run basedpyright` and fix all reported issues; treat LSP diagnostics as hints only (not a gate). Follow with `uv run ruff check .` before tests.

instincts.title: Prefer workflow command docs over duplicating skills in templates
instincts.trigger: When documenting workflows for end-users inside compound-installed `.opencode/` trees.
instincts.action: Put loom command guidance in `.opencode/commands/loom-*.md` (command-discoverable docs) and avoid duplicating full skill content inside the compound template unless required; keep skills procedural and source-of-truth in `.opencode/skills/` at repo root.

instincts.title: Run typecheck + lint gates before pytest
instincts.trigger: After non-trivial Python edits (new modules, refactors, deletes) and before running tests or claiming done
instincts.action: Run `uv run basedpyright` then `uv run ruff check .` and fix all issues before `uv run pytest`.

instincts.title: Prefer UX Tests Over Internal Coupling
instincts.trigger: When adding tests for command behavior
instincts.action: Test through the CLI boundary (command invocation) rather than deep internal functions; assert the minimal observable behavior that matters (selected lines, not full dumps).

instincts.title: Prompt/Template Tests Should Assert Structure, Not Full Text
instincts.trigger: Writing or updating tests that validate generated prompts, templates, or other large text blobs that can change with harmless wording edits.
instincts.action: Assert on stable structure: required sections/markers, presence of key instructions, and critical interpolated values. Avoid full-string snapshots unless the format is explicitly versioned; keep assertions minimal to reduce churn while preserving intent coverage.

instincts.title: Run python quality gates even when most changes are docs/skills
instincts.trigger: When a change set includes any Python edits (even small ones) alongside lots of markdown/skill edits
instincts.action: Run uv-based gates in order: `uv run ruff check .` then `uv run basedpyright` then relevant `uv run pytest` subset (at least pack tests if pack touched). Fix warnings/errors instead of deferring.

instincts.title: Run typecheck + lint gates before tests
instincts.trigger: After making Python code changes (or before declaring work done) in Loom
instincts.action: Run `uv run basedpyright` then `uv run ruff check .` and address issues; only then run `uv run pytest`

instincts.title: After deleting a module, systematically eliminate all references
instincts.trigger: A refactor removes/renames modules (large deletions or package reshapes)
instincts.action: Search for old module names and key symbols (e.g. with ripgrep/grep), update imports, docs, and tests together; ensure no dead entrypoints remain.

instincts.title: Refactors Must Carry Contract Tests Along
instincts.trigger: Moving responsibilities across modules/packages (e.g., installer logic, lifecycle logic, orchestration core) where behavior is user-facing or used by other subsystems.
instincts.action: Before considering the refactor complete, ensure there are explicit tests for the contract (inputs/outputs, idempotency, lifecycle transitions) and delete tests that target removed paths. Prefer small targeted tests over broad integration snapshots unless behavior is truly end-to-end.

instincts.title: Remove Orphaned Package Surface After Large Deletions
instincts.trigger: When removing many files or an entire subtree (e.g., `.../poly/*`)
instincts.action: Check `__init__.py` exports, public module surface, and docs/README links for orphans; ensure imports fail nowhere and no dead entrypoints remain.

instincts.title: Prefer reproducible packaging outputs
instincts.trigger: When implementing or extending `pack`-like functionality that builds archives/bundles/manifests
instincts.action: Sort input files, normalize path separators, and (when applicable) normalize archive metadata (mtime, uid/gid, mode) so repeated runs produce identical artifacts; document any intentional non-determinism explicitly.

instincts.title: Prefer explicit scope kind prefixes over heuristics
instincts.trigger: When accepting user-provided scope strings (CLI flags, config, or interactive prompts)
instincts.action: Require explicit prefixes like `file:...` / `command:...` / `dir:...` (or whatever set Loom supports) instead of trying to infer intent from the value; document the accepted prefixes and keep them minimal to reduce ambiguity and surprise.

instincts.title: Normalize path scopes cautiously and deterministically
instincts.trigger: When implementing path-like scopes (file/dir/repo-ish) or changing how they are serialized
instincts.action: Normalize only what is safe and deterministic (e.g., strip whitespace, unify separators if needed, avoid environment-dependent resolution); avoid implicit `resolve()` / cwd-dependent behavior unless the scope kind explicitly means 'relative to cwd'.

instincts.title: Treat scope parse/format as a strict roundtrip contract
instincts.trigger: When adding or modifying Loom memory scope parsing/formatting (especially new scope kinds or new accepted spellings)
instincts.action: Define an explicit internal representation for scopes and require `parse -> format -> parse` roundtrips to be stable; reject/raise on invalid inputs rather than guessing; keep one canonical string form for each scope to avoid drift in stored memory metadata.

instincts.title: Make scope validation errors actionable
instincts.trigger: When adding new validation rules to scopes
instincts.action: Raise errors that include: the original scope string, the expected grammar/prefix, and 1-2 example valid forms; ensure error text is stable enough for CLI UX tests.

instincts.title: Stabilize CLI Output Contracts
instincts.trigger: When printing structured lists/summaries in CLI
instincts.action: Ensure ordering and formatting are deterministic (sorting, fixed labels); avoid including volatile fields in user-facing output unless explicitly requested, and ensure tests lock the contract.

instincts.title: Subsystem Removal Requires Repo-Wide Sweep
instincts.trigger: When deleting or deprecating a substantial module/package (especially with many submodules)
instincts.action: Immediately sweep for imports/exports, docs references, CLI help/examples, tests, and any API schemas; remove or rewrite references so the repo remains coherent and discoverable.

instincts.title: Keep compound opencode plugin copies in sync
instincts.trigger: When editing the compound engineering opencode plugin (or any distributed opencode settings) and you notice there is both a repo-local copy and a distributed copy under src/
instincts.action: Make the exact same change in both `.opencode/plugins/compound_engineering.ts` and `src/agent_loom/compound/opencode/plugins/compound_engineering.ts`, and sanity-check diffs so they only diverge when intentionally versioned.

instincts.title: Keep distributed opencode plugin in sync
instincts.trigger: When editing `.opencode/plugins/compound_engineering.ts` or any opencode plugin that is also shipped under `src/agent_loom/**/opencode/plugins/`
instincts.action: Treat the plugin as having a source-of-truth and a distributed copy: update both copies in the same change, or consolidate to a single source and add a deterministic sync step + a check (e.g., CI/test that asserts the two files are identical) so drift can’t happen silently.

instincts.title: Use repo-root-relative paths in template docs
instincts.trigger: When editing README/docs content that will be scaffolded into a repo (e.g., src/agent_loom/compound/opencode/README.md, src/agent_loom/compound/opencode/.loom/compound/README.md, `.opencode/commands/*.md`).
instincts.action: Write links and references using repo-root-relative paths (no absolute machine paths, no editor URIs) and avoid references to removed/retired template files (e.g., AGENTS.md copies) to keep docs portable and deterministic.

instincts.title: Run uv-based gates before calling work done
instincts.trigger: After implementing user-visible CLI changes (even small ones)
instincts.action: Run `uv run ruff check .`, then `uv run basedpyright`, then `uv run pytest`; fix all warnings/errors (no waivers) before considering the change complete.
